title: Domain Evidence, Regulation, Standards, and Licensing Boundaries · Claim Defense
summary: A review board compares near-miss decisions for domain evidence, regulation, standards, and licensing boundaries and accepts only observable behavior, human evidence, explicit non-claims, and owned transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for domain authority and compliance boundary dossier. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish law, regulation, standard, specification, official guidance, contract, policy, research, convention, and opinion by jurisdiction and authority.
Record version, status, jurisdiction, applicability, review date, supersession, and monitoring owner for governing sources.
Identify decisions requiring legal, safety, clinical, financial, accessibility, security, privacy, or domain expert review.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# capstone-project cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: capstone-project
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Authority Hierarchy · changed case · Quiz Authority Hierarchy
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Secondary summaries, a draft framework, incompatible content licenses, and a broad compliance claim are treated as equal governing authority. The quiz variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Distinguish law, regulation, standard, specification, official guidance, contract, policy, research, convention, and opinion by jurisdiction and authority.

Which action demonstrates Authority Hierarchy without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: Authority Hierarchy · changed case · Quiz Authority Hierarchy.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish law, regulation, standard, specification, official guidance, contract, policy, research, convention, and opinion by jurisdiction and authority. For Authority Hierarchy · changed case · Quiz Authority Hierarchy, keep Authority Hierarchy connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: Secondary summaries, a draft framework, incompatible content licenses, and a broad compliance claim are treated as equal governing authority. The quiz variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Distinguish law, regulation, standard, specification, official guidance, contract, policy, research, convention, and opinion by jurisdiction and authority.

content.title: Evidence inspection
content.text: Use the supplied trace to distinguish an observed Authority Hierarchy result from an unsupported claim.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: GitHub healthy contribution and repository security documentation — Controls contribution guidance, governance, licensing, support, security policy, dependency review, code scanning, secret scanning, and repository stewardship.

At Authority Hierarchy · changed case · Quiz Authority Hierarchy, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: Secondary summaries, a draft framework, incompatible content licenses, and a broad compliance claim are treated as equal governing authority. The quiz variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Distinguish law, regulation, standard, specification, official guidance, contract, policy, research, convention, and opinion by jurisdiction and authority.

For Authority Hierarchy · changed case · Quiz Authority Hierarchy, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: All official-looking web pages impose equivalent requirements.

steps.instruction:

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Compliance Nonclaim
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Compliance Nonclaim: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Secondary summaries, a draft framework, incompatible content licenses, and a broad compliance claim are treated as equal governing authority. The quiz variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Separate implemented controls and collected evidence from formal compliance, certification, approval, or legal conclusions.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Separate implemented controls and collected evidence from formal compliance, certification, approval, or legal conclusions.
